headline: American Express Profit Jumps 15%. Affluent Spenders Are in Good Shape.
summary: American Express  said its profit increased by 15% from a year ago as spending by its typically affluent cardholder base remained robust.  Overall cardmembers’ spending, which American Express refers to as billed business, rose 9% on a foreign-exchange adjusted basis.  Amex has said in recent quarters that its clientele has kept spending while credit conditions, measured by write-off rates and other data, are healthy.

content.title: American Express Profit Jumps 15%. Affluent Spenders Are in Good Shape.
content.summary: American Express  said its profit increased by 15% from a year ago as spending by its typically affluent cardholder base remained robust.  Overall cardmembers’ spending, which American Express refers to as billed business, rose 9% on a foreign-exchange adjusted basis.  Amex has said in recent quarters that its clientele has kept spending while credit conditions, measured by write-off rates and other data, are healthy.

headline: American Express beats Q1 forecasts, maintains full-year guidance
summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) reported first-quarter results on Thursday that exceeded Wall Street expectations, supported by strong card spending, rising balances, and solid fee income growth. Shares of the payments company gained about 1% in premarket trading by 07:06 ET.

headline: American Express (NYSE:AXP) Surprises With Q1 CY2026 Sales
summary: Global payments company American Express (NYSE:AXP) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 19.5% year on year to $18.91 billion. Its GAAP profit of $4.28 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: American Express (NYSE:AXP) Surprises With Q1 CY2026 Sales
content.summary: Global payments company American Express (NYSE:AXP) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 19.5% year on year to $18.91 billion. Its GAAP profit of $4.28 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Warner Bros. shareholders approve $110B sale to Paramount
summary: Yahoo Finance's Brooke DiPalma and Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday's trending tickers and stories. Warner Bros. (WBD) investors voted to approve the $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance (PSKY). American Airlines (AAL) cut its profit forecast due to higher fuel prices. American Express (AXP) stock is moving lower, even after the company's earnings results beat analyst estimates.

content.title: Warner Bros. shareholders approve $110B sale to Paramount
content.description:
Yahoo Finance's Brooke DiPalma and Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday's trending tickers and stories.

Warner Bros. (WBD) investors voted to approve the $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance (PSKY).

American Airlines (AAL) cut its profit forecast due to higher fuel prices.

American Express (AXP) stock is moving lower, even after the company's earnings results beat analyst estimates.
content.summary: Yahoo Finance's Brooke DiPalma and Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday's trending tickers and stories. Warner Bros. (WBD) investors voted to approve the $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance (PSKY). American Airlines (AAL) cut its profit forecast due to higher fuel prices. American Express (AXP) stock is moving lower, even after the company's earnings results beat analyst estimates.

headline: American Express (AXP), The Longest-Held Position of Warren Buffett; See Why
summary: American Express Company (NYSE:AXP) is one of the best Warren Buffett stocks. During the initial wave of consumer credit in the 1960s, American Express Company (NYSE:AXP) drew Buffett’s attention, prompting his acquisition of a 5% stake in the company. The company remains one of Berkshire Hathaway’s longest-held positions, appreciating significantly over the years. As of […]

summary: The stock is down 3% on the day despite a mostly positive report.

headline: Amex (AXP) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Stephen Squeri: Thank you, Kartik.  Card Member spending grew 10% on a reported basis, and this is the highest quarterly growth in three years, driven by strong growth across both goods and services and T&E. We continue to see strong demand and engagement with our premium products across our customer base.  Based on our strong results to date and our confidence going forward, we have decided to increase our investments in marketing and technology to capitalize on key growth opportunities and build on our momentum.

content.title: Amex (AXP) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
content.summary: Stephen Squeri: Thank you, Kartik.  Card Member spending grew 10% on a reported basis, and this is the highest quarterly growth in three years, driven by strong growth across both goods and services and T&E. We continue to see strong demand and engagement with our premium products across our customer base.  Based on our strong results to date and our confidence going forward, we have decided to increase our investments in marketing and technology to capitalize on key growth opportunities and build on our momentum.
